subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Coma Cluster and Dark Matter
description: A 60-second lesson on The Coma Cluster and Dark Matter.

hook.text: The Coma Cluster was the first place dark matter was proposed. Galaxies inside it moved far too fast for their visible mass.

buildup.text: Fritz Zwicky measured galaxy speeds in 1933 and found them 10 times faster than visible matter could explain.

discovery.text: He called the missing stuff 'dunkle Materie'—dark matter. The idea was ignored for nearly 40 years.

twist.text: Vera Rubin's galaxy rotation work in the 1970s revived the concept. Zwicky had been right all along but too far ahead of his time.

climax.text: Today, the Coma Cluster is a key target for mapping dark matter with gravitational lensing.

punchline.text: Too fast for their mass. Zwicky saw it first. The world took 40 years to listen.

quiz.question: What did Fritz Zwicky discover about the Coma Cluster in 1933?
options: Galaxies moved too fast—visible matter alone couldn't hold the cluster together
The cluster was expanding faster than the rest of the universe
All galaxies in the cluster had supermassive black holes
